title: A possible explanation for Shinji
wikitext:
[]: A possible explanation for Shinji             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri Mar 20, 2009 6:37 pm Last night for a bit of fun I was looking on a site http://www.tshirthell.com/ to be specific. Flicking through the images I cam across this. Now I know a few of you here are so I am not poking fun with this at all but there is a surprising resemblance here. So I guess thats the Question. Is Shinji Autistic? Maybe not fully but does show signs of his wanting to be alone. Finding it hard to make friends and remaining Quiet.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Mar 20, 2009 6:48 pm No. Wanting to be alone and having difficulty interacting with individuals is not the same as having autism. Shinji's issues are probably psychological injuries brought on by the sudden loss of both parents- note that he has definitely blocked the memories of mom's death and he denies his father's responsibility in their seperation. These issues, plus a sudden change in location, probably led to his being withdrawn, and he lacked the environment with which to get acceptance of the loss of parents, both of which meant that he missed social development with children. Shinji shows no signs of improvement until he begins to interact with Rei (an even more withdrawn individual) and Kensuke explains to him that he and Misato are a family, at which point his self-imposed exile begins to end. Asuka's presence helps him as well, until the arrival of Leliel, at which point he was constantly psychologically stressed and began to fall back into himself. Kaworu attempts to pull him out of this spiral of depression, but fails due to his nature, and it is only the events of End that give Shinji the context with which to handle the world. But there's no autism involved.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri Mar 20, 2009 6:53 pm I read the book "the curios incident of the dog in the night time" a while back. He shows many signs relating to the way the character behaves. I understand he does improve with the interaction of other people but so does the character in the book. I wasn’t suggesting full autism but only on the smallest level. I guess his interaction with Rei is he feels comfortable around her because she reminds him of Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Fri Mar 20, 2009 6:58 pm http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taijin_kyofusho

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 20, 2009 7:11 pm Zeak wrote: I wasn’t suggesting full autism but only on the smallest level.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Mar 20, 2009 7:25 pm Reichu wrote: Also, what Legendary said.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Fri Mar 20, 2009 7:30 pm Reichu's pretty easily mollified. You just pet her head and dangle her toy mouse in front of her.

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Fri Mar 20, 2009 7:32 pm I wish most doctors felt like Reichu. Then I wouldn't have to always hear crap like "autism up 200% in ten years" on the news. Well yeah, when you keep making up new types of autism. Besides, this is the possible explanation for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri Mar 20, 2009 8:06 pm Fair enough. I see what you mean. LMOA thats a win.

[]:             Quote Post by Mackerni » Sat Mar 21, 2009 12:03 am Most people would not admit that they are like any of the characters in Evangelion. Especially Shinji... Unfortunately, I'm not most people. I'm a LOT like Shinji. The more I use 'labels' to see who I am, the more I become that label, and the less likely I am able to move on from that label. I've been called everything from schizoid to social personality disorder to Asperger's to depression and bi-polar. So just calling him one of these would probably not be right... But I think the one thing I've learned about it all is that there are two kinds of mental conditions: the ones that people inherently act and feel and the ones that are developed throughout our lives (like the difference between someone who is born retarded or someone who gets in an car accident and has a coma, to name polar opposites). Shinji, like all the characters in Evangelion, live to live. He was not born with an inherent disorder - as this was completely illustrated by the original ending and the alternate reality Shinji was in. This is what makes the characters so fascinating - they all showed the basic desire to be the best of who they truly are, but for some reason or another that became corrupted to the point that many of them, including Shinji, didn't want to live life anymore. The fascination part comes in when we can look upon those characters and be like, "yeah, I'm like that", when it comes to all the positive attributes of the characters, as negative as NGE shows these characters out to be, you can look upon them in a different light: Asuka is proud of herself, Shinji (almost) always does what he thinks is morally right, Rei is obedient to Gendo, Misato is focused at her work, Kaji is easygoing, Ritsuko is intelligent, and Gendo even try to plea for forgiveness right before Eva 01 ate him. Evangelion's characters are pretty stereotyped, even for the Western Hemisphere. Rei is the shy girl, Asuka is horny 'OMG I'm not a slut' slut, Misato is the fixed but not focused character, Shinji be the self-conscious hero... You get the point.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sat Mar 21, 2009 6:36 am How about aspergers? Everyone else seems to have it nowadays. It's kinda like autism-lite isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 21, 2009 1:25 pm master_lloyd wrote: How about aspergers? ... It's kinda like autism-lite isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by GVman » Sat Mar 21, 2009 8:15 pm Did Rei I line her toys up in a line? Constantly make towers of blocks? Memorize the species classifications of dinosaurs?

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Sat Mar 21, 2009 9:17 pm I did... What are you getting at?

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sun Mar 22, 2009 7:28 am GVman wrote: Did Rei I line her toys up in a line? Constantly make towers of blocks? Memorize the species classifications of dinosaurs?

[]:             Quote Post by GVman » Sun Mar 22, 2009 8:19 am She'd be the cutest little autistic girl ever. We need some fanart of this.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sun Mar 22, 2009 8:26 am Shouldnt be pokeing fun but that sounds very funny.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Mar 22, 2009 11:09 am Actually, since there's some (flimsy) theories that autism might be a primordial "default" for human consciousness and might've been responsible for our leap into using tools, it would be pretty appropriate for Rei I to be moderately autistic. Hur hur fruit of knowledge. Shinji, however, is not autistic. Not one bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Mar 23, 2009 9:44 am Legendary wrote: note that he has definitely blocked the memories of mom's death and he denies his father's responsibility in their seperation.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Mar 23, 2009 3:08 pm Shinji shows way too much affect to be autistic. He is at best depressed and at worst dissociated at his nadir.
